commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Weather condition emoji (line 72) lacks semantic meaning — wrap in span with aria-label describing the weather condition, e.g., aria-label='Sunny weather'
issues.fix: Weather condition emoji (line 72) lacks semantic meaning — wrap in span with aria-label describing the weather condition, e.g., aria-label='Sunny weather'

issues.description: Forecast day emoji icons (line 111) lack aria-labels — each should have aria-label describing the weather condition (e.g., aria-label='Sunny weather')
issues.fix: Forecast day emoji icons (line 111) lack aria-labels — each should have aria-label describing the weather condition (e.g., aria-label='Sunny weather')

issues.description: Icon component (lines 81, 89) should verify it has aria-label or is purely decorative — if decorative, add aria-hidden='true'
issues.fix: Icon component (lines 81, 89) should verify it has aria-label or is purely decorative — if decorative, add aria-hidden='true'

issues.description: Heading hierarchy uses h4 (level={4} on line 103) when h2 is already used (line 62) — should use h3 (level={3}) for proper hierarchy
issues.fix: Heading hierarchy uses h4 (level={4} on line 103) when h2 is already used (line 62) — should use h3 (level={3}) for proper hierarchy

issues.description: Temperature display (line 68-69) shows only a number without semantic context — consider wrapping in a section with aria-label='Current temperature' for clarity
issues.fix: Temperature display (line 68-69) shows only a number without semantic context — consider wrapping in a section with aria-label='Current temperature' for clarity

issues.fix: Inconsistent card shadows: main weather card uses shadow='md' (32px padding) while forecast card uses shadow='sm' (20px padding) — should maintain visual hierarchy consistency

issues.description: Forecast day cards use inline style with calc() for flex basis instead of design system spacing tokens — should use standardized gap and width utilities
issues.fix: Forecast day cards use inline style with calc() for flex basis instead of design system spacing tokens — should use standardized gap and width utilities

issues.description: Humidity/Wind Speed section in Row uses gap={24} but inner Stack labels use gap={2} — spacing ratios are inconsistent across vertical/horizontal axes
issues.fix: Humidity/Wind Speed section in Row uses gap={24} but inner Stack labels use gap={2} — spacing ratios are inconsistent across vertical/horizontal axes

issues.description: No visual distinction between the main weather card and forecast section — consider using different background colors or subtle borders to separate content zones
issues.fix: No visual distinction between the main weather card and forecast section — consider using different background colors or subtle borders to separate content zones

issues.description: Forecast cards (line 108) have minWidth='80px' as inline style instead of using design system size token — breaks consistency with spacing system
issues.fix: Forecast cards (line 108) have minWidth='80px' as inline style instead of using design system size token — breaks consistency with spacing system

issues.description: Divider on line 76 uses margin={16} but surrounding Stacks use gap={24} — inconsistent vertical rhythm
issues.fix: Divider on line 76 uses margin={16} but surrounding Stacks use gap={24} — inconsistent vertical rhythm

issues.description: Empty forecast array has no empty state message — when props.forecast is empty or only has partial data, user sees nothing instead of 'No forecast available' or similar message
issues.fix: Empty forecast array has no empty state message — when props.forecast is empty or only has partial data, user sees nothing instead of 'No forecast available' or similar message

issues.description: Missing validation for required props (city, temperature, condition, humidity, windSpeed) — component renders even if critical weather data is undefined or null, potentially showing blank values
issues.fix: Missing validation for required props (city, temperature, condition, humidity, windSpeed) — component renders even if critical weather data is undefined or null, potentially showing blank values

issues.fix: Line 108: Inline style object 'flex: "1 1 calc(20% - 10px)", minWidth: "80px"' bypasses design system — should use spacing tokens and Stack/Row layout props instead

issues.fix: Line 108: 'border={true}' used without specifying border color or style — should use design system border tokens (e.g., border color from var(--ggui-color-border))

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

The code is cut off mid-step-3, making it impossible to evaluate the complete implementation including step 4 and navigation polish.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

issues.description: Task title editing does not support double-click activation — only manual click activation on the card body is implemented
issues.fix: Task title editing does not support double-click activation — only manual click activation on the card body is implemented

issues.description: Task edit button is missing — the code only shows the move dropdown in non-editing mode but lacks a dedicated edit button as specified in requirements
issues.fix: Task edit button is missing — the code only shows the move dropdown in non-editing mode but lacks a dedicated edit button as specified in requirements

issues.description: New tasks should start in 'To Do' column by default — currently they are added to whichever column's 'Add Task' button was clicked, allowing creation in any column
issues.fix: New tasks should start in 'To Do' column by default — currently they are added to whichever column's 'Add Task' button was clicked, allowing creation in any column

issues.description: Task title edit action does not include title in the taskUpdate action payload — line 116 sends column and title to taskUpdate but the ActionTaskUpdatePayload type (line 17) only accepts column in data, causing payload mismatch
issues.fix: Task title edit action does not include title in the taskUpdate action payload — line 116 sends column and title to taskUpdate but the ActionTaskUpdatePayload type (line 17) only accepts column in data, causing payload mismatch

issues.description: Stream event handling for task creation doesn't send taskUpdate action — when other users create tasks via stream, the component merges them into state but does not fire taskUpdate (should mirror local task creation behavior)
issues.fix: Stream event handling for task creation doesn't send taskUpdate action — when other users create tasks via stream, the component merges them into state but does not fire taskUpdate (should mirror local task creation behavior)

issues.description: Line 116: taskUpdate action payload is missing required 'column' field in data object — should pass { column: col, title: editingTitle } but only passes { column: col } with incomplete structure
issues.fix: Line 116: taskUpdate action payload is missing required 'column' field in data object — should pass { column: col, title: editingTitle } but only passes { column: col } with incomplete structure

issues.description: Line 137: taskUpdate action payload structure mismatch — passes { column: columnId, title: newTask.title, priority: newTaskPriority } but data should only contain { column } per ActionTaskUpdatePayload type definition
issues.fix: Line 137: taskUpdate action payload structure mismatch — passes { column: columnId, title: newTask.title, priority: newTaskPriority } but data should only contain { column } per ActionTaskUpdatePayload type definition

issues.description: Line 98: Unsafe optional chaining on array index — (props.columns || [])[2]?.id could crash if columns is not properly initialized or has fewer than 3 items, should add fallback logic
issues.fix: Line 98: Unsafe optional chaining on array index — (props.columns || [])[2]?.id could crash if columns is not properly initialized or has fewer than 3 items, should add fallback logic

issues.description: Line 200: colTasks assignment uses optional fallback || [] which is correct, but accessing task properties in map without null guards — task.id, task.title, task.priority could be undefined since Props interface marks all properties as optional with ?
issues.fix: Line 200: colTasks assignment uses optional fallback || [] which is correct, but accessing task properties in map without null guards — task.id, task.title, task.priority could be undefined since Props interface marks all properties as optional with ?

issues.fix: Move dropdown trigger uses icon-only button without aria-label or title attribute — unclear purpose to screen reader users

issues.fix: Dropdown component (line 307) lacks semantic ARIA attributes for button toggle role and expanded state

issues.fix: No empty state message when a column has zero tasks — should display 'No tasks' or similar in empty columns to improve UX

issues.description: No loading state indicator for initial data or stream updates — component should show Spinner while tasks are being loaded or synchronized
issues.fix: No loading state indicator for initial data or stream updates — component should show Spinner while tasks are being loaded or synchronized

issues.description: Props data is accepted without validation — component assumes props.tasks and props.columns are always defined; missing null/undefined guard could cause issues if props are not provided on initial render
issues.fix: Props data is accepted without validation — component assumes props.tasks and props.columns are always defined; missing null/undefined guard could cause issues if props are not provided on initial render

issues.description: Line 208-214: Inline style object with raw flex values and display properties — should use design system spacing tokens and layout utilities instead of hardcoded 'flex: "1 1 280px"', 'minWidth: "280px"', 'maxWidth: "400px"'
issues.fix: Line 208-214: Inline style object with raw flex values and display properties — should use design system spacing tokens and layout utilities instead of hardcoded 'flex: "1 1 280px"', 'minWidth: "280px"', 'maxWidth: "400px"'

issues.description: Line 217: margin style uses fallback syntax 'var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)' which mixes CSS variable with hardcoded pixel value — should rely on design token only
issues.fix: Line 217: margin style uses fallback syntax 'var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)' which mixes CSS variable with hardcoded pixel value — should rely on design token only

issues.description: Line 227-233: Inline style object with raw overflow, padding, and minHeight values — 'overflowY: "auto"', 'paddingRight: "var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"', 'minHeight: "300px"' should use consistent spacing tokens without fallbacks
issues.fix: Line 227-233: Inline style object with raw overflow, padding, and minHeight values — 'overflowY: "auto"', 'paddingRight: "var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"', 'minHeight: "300px"' should use consistent spacing tokens without fallbacks

issues.description: Line 327: marginTop uses fallback syntax 'var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)' — should use consistent design token reference
issues.fix: Line 327: marginTop uses fallback syntax 'var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)' — should use consistent design token reference

issues.description: Line 252: pressStyle with 'transform: scale(0.98)' and 'opacity: 0.9' are custom values not from design system — should verify these match design token values for interaction feedback
issues.fix: Line 252: pressStyle with 'transform: scale(0.98)' and 'opacity: 0.9' are custom values not from design system — should verify these match design token values for interaction feedback

issues.description: Line 41: Props are directly assigned to state without merge logic. When props.tasks updates, it completely replaces local state, losing any in-flight edits or newly created tasks that haven't been synced back from the server.
issues.fix: Line 41: Props are directly assigned to state without merge logic. When props.tasks updates, it completely replaces local state, losing any in-flight edits or newly created tasks that haven't been synced back from the server.

issues.description: Line 59: Edit action uses shallow merge (...taskChangeEvent.task!) which can overwrite local edits. If a user is editing a task title locally while a stream edit event arrives for the same task, the stream event will overwrite local changes if they haven't been saved.
issues.fix: Line 59: Edit action uses shallow merge (...taskChangeEvent.task!) which can overwrite local edits. If a user is editing a task title locally while a stream edit event arrives for the same task, the stream event will overwrite local changes if they haven't been saved.

issues.description: Line 74: Event listener dependency is [editingTaskId], but it should be [] (empty). Current dependency causes the listener to be re-added whenever editingTaskId changes, creating multiple listeners and potential race conditions.
issues.fix: Line 74: Event listener dependency is [editingTaskId], but it should be [] (empty). Current dependency causes the listener to be re-added whenever editingTaskId changes, creating multiple listeners and potential race conditions.

issues.description: Lines 63-66: Cleanup of editing state when a task is deleted uses closure over editingTaskId state variable, but because the listener is re-created on every editingTaskId change, this cleanup may fire with stale editingTaskId values.
issues.fix: Lines 63-66: Cleanup of editing state when a task is deleted uses closure over editingTaskId state variable, but because the listener is re-created on every editingTaskId change, this cleanup may fire with stale editingTaskId values.

issues.description: Line 104: handleMoveTask optimistically updates local state but doesn't check for concurrent stream updates. If a stream 'move' event arrives before taskUpdate completes, both local and stream updates could create duplicate renders or conflicting states.
issues.fix: Line 104: handleMoveTask optimistically updates local state but doesn't check for concurrent stream updates. If a stream 'move' event arrives before taskUpdate completes, both local and stream updates could create duplicate renders or conflicting states.

issues.description: Line 115: handleSaveEdit updates local state and calls taskUpdate but doesn't lock editing state. A stream 'edit' event arriving during save could overwrite the user's edit with remote data.
issues.fix: Line 115: handleSaveEdit updates local state and calls taskUpdate but doesn't lock editing state. A stream 'edit' event arriving during save could overwrite the user's edit with remote data.

issues.description: Lines 51-54 and 57-59: Create and move operations don't use optimistic locking or version tracking. Rapid local and remote edits to the same task can result in lost updates or renders with inconsistent data.
issues.fix: Lines 51-54 and 57-59: Create and move operations don't use optimistic locking or version tracking. Rapid local and remote edits to the same task can result in lost updates or renders with inconsistent data.

issues.description: Line 41: No debouncing or batching of props.tasks changes. If props are updated with multiple task changes, each update to setTasks triggers a full re-render before the next update arrives, causing rendering thrashing.
issues.fix: Line 41: No debouncing or batching of props.tasks changes. If props are updated with multiple task changes, each update to setTasks triggers a full re-render before the next update arrives, causing rendering thrashing.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid state management foundation but suffers from incomplete implementation: the JSX rendering is cut off mid-component, missing the actual Kanban columns, task cards, and move/edit UI. It uses some design tokens but mixes them with hardcoded values (e.g., gap={16}, padding={16} as raw pixels). The logic for task grouping and editing is sound, but without the full render output, layout, polish, and visual hierarchy cannot be properly evaluated.
